items.title: Dominick Barlow joined the Sixers in the middle of summer league last year, his first step to becoming the team’s 2025-26 surprise.
items.author: Gina Mizell
items.body_excerpt:
Dominick Barlow joined the Sixers in the middle of summer league last year, his first step to becoming the team’s 2025-26 surprise. 

He is back in Vegas, and still embodying that path:

“You never know, one of these guys (could) be playing and helping us win.”

www.inquirer.com/sixers/domin...

media.title: Dominick Barlow is back in Las Vegas, as a success story for the summer Sixers
media.description: After joining the Sixers in Las Vegas last year, Barlow became a part-time starter, earned a standard contract, and had his $3.4 million team option for the 2026-27 season picked up.

items.title: Today the Summer Sixers face the Pacers, and former two-way player Alex Reese.
items.author: Gina Mizell
items.body_excerpt:
Today the Summer Sixers face the Pacers, and former two-way player Alex Reese. 

Do you remember him and his unconventional jersey number? Last season, @adamaaronson.bsky.social and I happened to ask about it, and he told us both about his mom. 

www.inquirer.com/sixers/alex-...

media.title: Alex Reese wears No. 65 to honor his late mother, including in the Pacers’ Summer League game vs. his old team
media.description: Reese, who played 14 games with the Sixers two seasons ago, chose the number because it aligns with the birth year of his mother, Rebakah, who died in 2024 from early-onset Alzheimer's disease.

Wrote about the first Summer League game for the Pacers and, specifically, my curiosity with how often Braden Smith stayed low as the low-man, tagging and rotating to the rim, rather than off-ball switching. 

On whether he'll seek higher ground and other notes:
